Rationale: Load-bearing role across all phases. Foundation data model design (Phase 1), integration patterns (Phase 2-3), analytics strategy (Phase 4). Addresses Government Cloud org design, FedRAMP compliance architecture, multi-cloud integration.
Comments: Highest continuity requirement — same architect across all phases preferred to maintain architectural coherence. Knowledge transfer risk if architect changes mid-program.

Rationale: Phase 1 critical path: USDA ATO application (6-9 months), Shield encryption, Platform Event Monitoring, Login.gov SSO. Phase 2: PCI DSS validation for Worldpay tokenization. Addresses highest schedule risk.
Comments: Must have federal government experience (NIST 800-53 controls, ATO application process). Commercial-only security background insufficient for USDA CISO coordination.

Rationale: Phase 1 peak: Complex business rules engine for 6,000+ facilities (dynamic booking windows, conflict detection, Platform Events). Phase 2: Booking workflows, payment integration. Phase 3: Data migration scripts (21M+ records). Addresses highest technical risk.
Comments: Requires Apex governor limits expertise (Bulk API optimization, Platform Events async patterns). Junior developers insufficient for business rules complexity.

Rationale: Phase 2 critical: Custom LWC map component (Mapbox/ArcGIS 1m GSD imagery), white-label widgets, mobile-first responsive design. Phase 3: Business rule configuration UI. Addresses highest UI complexity + Section 508 compliance.
Comments: Must have experience with third-party JavaScript library integration (Mapbox/ArcGIS APIs) and Section 508 accessibility patterns.

Rationale: Phase 2: Digital Experiences public portal (48M visitors, guest users, Login.gov SSO). Phase 3: Partner Community for 5,000 internal stakeholders (8 agencies, role-based access). Addresses 2 of 3 XL epics.
Comments: Must distinguish Digital Experiences (public, guest users) from Partner Community (authenticated, role-based) — different licensing models and design patterns.

Rationale: Phase 1: Login.gov SAML 2.0 SSO, agency IdP OAuth/SAML for MFA. Phase 2: Worldpay REST API, US Bank Lockbox SFTP, Service Cloud Voice Amazon Connect. Phase 3: Data Cloud connectors for 8 agency CMS. Addresses 10+ external integrations.
Comments: Requires OAuth 2.0, SAML 2.0, SFTP, and REST API experience. Government SSO integration (Login.gov) is atypical — commercial SSO patterns do not directly apply.

Rationale: Phase 2 only: Omni-Channel contact center (phone via Service Cloud Voice + Amazon Connect, chat, email), case management workflows, Knowledge Base. Addresses contact center requirement for 365-day operations.
Comments: Service Cloud Voice telephony setup is complex (Amazon Connect partner integration); requires prior Voice implementation experience. Generic Service Cloud knowledge insufficient.

Rationale: Phase 3 only: RIDB aggregation hub — connector setup for 8 agency CMS, data prep recipes, Data Cloud Connect REST API. Addresses newer Salesforce product learning curve and multi-agency schema harmonization.
Comments: Data Cloud is distinct from Platform (different data model, different query language). Requires Data Cloud certification or equivalent hands-on experience.

Rationale: Phase 3 only: Offline mobile app for field users at 6,000+ remote facilities — local storage (SQLite), sync conflict resolution, connectivity monitoring. Addresses offline sync conflict risk.
Comments: Salesforce Mobile SDK is distinct from generic mobile development. Must have prior SDK offline sync experience — no native conflict resolution exists.

Rationale: Phase 3 critical path: Phased migration strategy for 21M+ reservations, 6,000+ facilities, 10M+ user profiles. Coordinates incumbent contractor per SOO Section 6.3, designs parallel operations validation. Addresses highest data risk.
Comments: Requires Salesforce Bulk API expertise and federal contractor transition-out experience. Commercial data migration patterns insufficient for incumbent coordination complexity.

Rationale: Phase 3 only: Business rules reverse-engineering, data cleansing, MuleSoft/Heroku setup for 21M+ records. Addresses incumbent contractor documentation gaps and ETL tool complexity.
Comments: MuleSoft vs. Heroku decision deferred to Phase 1 (architecture decision). Either tool requires Salesforce connector experience and Bulk API optimization.

Rationale: Phase 3 only: Validate 21M+ migrated records (automated + manual spot checks), identify duplicates/missing fields. Designs data quality checkpoints per migration phase. Addresses <1% error rate target.
Comments: Requires Salesforce data model knowledge (standard vs. custom objects, required fields, validation rules) and statistical sampling techniques for 21M+ record validation.

Rationale: Phase 4 only: CRM Analytics (Tableau) embedded dashboards for PMO, APMs, public insights. Standard QASP reports, ad-hoc query builder. Addresses QASP surveillance requirement and monthly PMR cadence.
Comments: CRM Analytics (Tableau) is distinct from Reports & Dashboards (different data model, different query language). Requires Tableau CRM certification or equivalent.

Rationale: Phase 4 only: Multi-tiered training programs (APMs, field staff, concessionaires, public) and train-the-trainer (2-3 champions per agency). Addresses Government user engagement (5,000 stakeholders).
Comments: Requires adult learning principles expertise and train-the-trainer facilitation experience. Generic Salesforce training insufficient for government user audience (federal compliance context).

Rationale: Phase 1: Government Cloud org setup, GitHub initialization. Phase 4: DevOps Center configuration, deployment pipeline, CI/CD training for Government IT. Addresses government ownership requirement (SOO Section 6.1).
Comments: Salesforce DevOps Center is newer product (distinct from Jenkins/GitHub Actions patterns). Requires DevOps Center hands-on experience or willingness to learn during Phase 1.

Rationale: Phase 2 only: Section 508/WCAG Level AA compliance testing for public portal — screen reader validation, keyboard navigation, color contrast. Conducts audits at sprint 12 and sprint 18 (pre-UAT). Addresses SOO mandatory 508 requirement.
Comments: Requires Section 508 certification or equivalent (WCAG 2.1 Level AA). Generic QA testing insufficient — accessibility testing is specialized discipline (assistive technology usage patterns).

Rationale: All phases: Phase 1 unit testing (85%+ Apex coverage, business rules regression). Phase 2 load testing (48M visitor capacity). Phase 3 data validation (21M+ records). Phase 4 UAT coordination. Addresses complexity and performance risk.
Comments: Requires Salesforce-specific testing tools knowledge (Apex Test Framework, Salesforce CLI, JMeter for load testing). Generic software QA background insufficient for Apex governor limits testing.

Rationale: All phases: Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, demos, retrospectives), QASP surveillance (monthly metrics for quarterly PMRs), Government stakeholder coordination (USDA CISO, APMs, field users). Addresses SOO Section 6.2 Agile requirement.
Comments: Requires federal government program management experience (quarterly PMR format, QASP surveillance, ATO coordination). Commercial Scrum Master background insufficient for government stakeholder coordination complexity.
